and, Patrick Chao, Andrew Fearing, John Lin, Simin Liu, Grant Walton, Yuxiang Yang 2016-present: Liyu Wang 2015 and before: Duncan Haldane and anonymous students Overview The OpenRoACH is a 15-cm long and 200-gram hexapod crawling robot. As the first

and 200-gram hexapod crawling robot. As the first documented open-source legged robot platform, it is affordable with accessible materials and off-the-shelf components. Additionally, fabrication is relative easy and straightforward with bench-top fa

elative easy and straightforward with bench-top fast-prototyping machines such as a laser cutter and a 3D printer. Assembly can be done manually with hot glue, super glue and screws. The present version is version 4.0. Mechanical Design and Fabricati

on is version 4.0. Mechanical Design and Fabrication CAD files (Andrew) Fabrication process (John) Assembly guide (Patrick) Fabrication Instructions Sensors Line scanner sensor Line Scanner Proximity sensor and electromagnet Light Sensor and Electrom

sensor and electromagnet Light Sensor and Electromagnet Electronics and Microcontrollers Locomotion Control Version history v4.0: July 2017-August 2017 Chassis middle lowered to accommodate electronics. Tested higher speed motors, 50:1 Micro Metal Ge

s. Tested higher speed motors, 50:1 Micro Metal Gearmotor HP. v3.0: June 2017-July 2017 Legs from C-shape to O-shape. Two-piece 3D-printable pulleys and an improved 3D-printable motor carrier.\\ Reuse the initial motor driver on Zumy board, remove m

e the initial motor driver on Zumy board, remove many wiring cables. v2.0: February 2017-May 2017 Chassis made from paperboard, nylon and PET. Dual shafts, 298:1 Micro Metal Gearmotor HP, three-piece 3D-printable pulleys, timing belts, and a 3D-print

3D-printable pulleys, timing belts, and a 3D-printable motor carrier.\\ Self-contained with 22-gram battery. v1.5: November 2016 - January 2017 Chassis made from PET and nylon. Leg transmission bar made from Delrin. Self-contained with onboard Zumy

ade from Delrin. Self-contained with onboard Zumy board, Dual MC33926 Motor Driver Carrier, 50-gram battery, and line scanner sensor. Realized path following with ∞ trajectory three times. v1.0: Before October 2016 Only a 1.5x scaled VelociRoACH chas

e October 2016 Only a 1.5x scaled VelociRoACH chassis made from paperboard and nylon with a Tamiya twin motor gearbox. AutoCAD drawings, assembly guide, and bill of materials for v1.0 are available on Github. Wishlist Demonstrate OpenRoACH working wi
